title: Diosma biseriata
taxonomy: Diosma biseriata is classified within the family Rutaceae, which comprises plants commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This classification places Diosma biseriata among other plants such as citrus trees and rue species. Diosma biseriata is a member of the genus Diosma, which is native to the mountainous regions of South Africa. The Rutaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes. Diosma biseriata's classification is an essential aspect of understanding its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

intro: Diosma biseriata is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as a shrub with medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, and its extracts have been reported to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Diosma biseriata is rich in bioactive constituents, including sesquiterpenoids and limonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Diosma biseriata is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves for wound healing and skin care.
